issues.claim: Analog does not equally apply elsewhere
issues.objection: Force-fit test for 7.22: the analog (thread 3 (faith-made-firm continues; granting-agency is upstream)) is anchored to the verse's specific structural claim; it would not apply to a randomly chosen alternate verse. The verse's central image — for 7.22, the image specifically tied to thread 3 (faith-made-firm continues; granting-agency is upstream) — is what the engineering scenario depends on; substituting another verse's image breaks the analog. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims Krishna is the actual granter (mayaiva vihitān); engineering analog operates at proximate-vs-upstream attribution scope (the framework is the vehicle; the customer-need-and-substrate is what actually grants the result). Counter-example carves out accidental success without disciplined practice.

notes: Force-fit test for 7.22: the engineering analog cannot equally well derive from a different verse. Tags (operator-system-coupling, essence-vs-implementation, deliberate-action) are anchored on this verse's specific claim. PASS.
